quality.rationale: 多步推导（测地线频率+局部正交标架+电磁场洛伦兹boost）+明确RN度规背景+标准GR理论场景

origin.question: Problem 17.5. An observer in a circular orbit of circumference $2\pi r$ around a charged, spherical, black hole (a Reissner-Nordstrom black hole) of mass M and charge Q measures local electric and magnetic fields. What are their strengths and orientations?
origin.answer: 强度：$E_{\\hat{r}} = \\frac{Q}{r^2} \\left(\\frac{r^2 - 2Mr + Q^2}{r^2 - 3Mr + 2Q^2}\\right)^{1/2}$，$B_{\\hat{\\theta}} = \\frac{Q}{r^2} \\left(\\frac{Mr - Q^2}{r^2 - 3Mr + 2Q^2}\\right)^{1/2}$；方向：电场沿径向（$\\hat{r}$），磁场沿极向（$\\hat{\\theta}$）。
origin.solution:
Solution 17.5. The Reissner-Nordström metric is
$$
\mathrm {d s}^{2} = - \mathrm {A d t}^{2} + \mathrm {A}^{- 1} \mathrm {d r}^{2} + \mathrm {r}^{2} (\mathrm {d} \theta^{2} + \sin^{2} \theta \mathrm {d} \phi^{2})
$$
where $\mathbf{A} \equiv 1 - 2\mathbf{M} / \mathbf{r} + \mathbf{Q}^2 / \mathbf{r}^2$ . We first find the Keplerian frequency $\Omega \equiv  rac{\mathrm{d}\phi}{\mathrm{d}t}$ of circular orbits, in the same manner as in Solution 17.4. Here
$$
\Gamma_{\mathrm {r t t}} = -  rac {1}{2} \mathrm {g}_{\mathrm {t t}, \mathrm {r}} =  rac {\mathrm {M}}{\mathrm {r}^{2}} -  rac {\mathrm {Q}^{2}}{\mathrm {r}^{3}}
$$
$$
2 \Gamma_{\mathrm {r} t \phi} = - \mathbf {g}_{\mathrm {t} \phi , \mathrm {r}} = 0
$$
$$
\Gamma_{r \phi \phi} = -  rac {1}{2} g_{\phi \phi , r} = - r
$$
so the quadratic equation for $\Omega$ becomes
$$
\Omega^{2} \mathrm {r} - \left( rac {\mathrm {M}}{\mathrm {r}^{2}} -  rac {\mathrm {Q}^{2}}{\mathrm {r}^{3}}ight) = 0
$$
and the orbital frequency is
$$
\Omega = \pm \left( rac {M}{r^{3}} -  rac {Q^{2}}{r^{4}}ight)^{ rac {1}{2}}.
$$
The proper velocity of the orbiting observer relative to a coordinate stationary observer is
$$
\hat {\mathrm {v}} =  rac {\mathrm {d} \hat {\phi}}{\mathrm {d} \hat {\mathrm {t}}} =  rac {\mathrm {r} \mathrm {d} \phi}{\mathrm {A}^{ rac {1}{2}} \mathrm {d t}} = \left( rac {\mathrm {M r} - \mathrm {Q}^{2}}{\mathrm {r}^{2} - 2 \mathrm {M r} + \mathrm {Q}^{2}}ight)^{ rac {1}{2}}.
$$
(Carets indicate orthonormal components in the stationary frame.) In the stationary frame the local electromagnetic field has only the component
$$
E_{\mathrm {r}} =  rac {Q}{\mathrm {r}^{2}}.
$$
Lorentz transforming this with the standard relations for E-M fields gives, in the orbiting frame, all components zero except
$$
E_{\mathrm {r}} = (1 - \hat {\mathrm {v}}^{2})^{-  rac {1}{2}}  rac {Q}{\mathrm {r}^{2}} =  rac {Q}{\mathrm {r}^{2}} \left( rac {\mathrm {r}^{2} - 2 \mathrm {M r} + Q^{2}}{\mathrm {r}^{2} - 3 \mathrm {M r} + 2 Q^{2}}ight)^{ rac {1}{2}}
$$
$$
\mathbf {B}_{\hat {	heta}} = (1 - \hat {\mathbf {v}}^{2})^{-  rac {1}{2}} \hat {\mathbf {v}}  rac {\mathrm {Q}}{\mathrm {r}^{2}} =  rac {\mathrm {Q}}{\mathrm {r}^{2}} \left( rac {\mathrm {M r} - \mathrm {Q}^{2}}{\mathrm {r}^{2} - 3 \mathrm {M r} + 2 \mathrm {Q}^{2}}ight)^{ rac {1}{2}}.
$$

killing_vectors.name: time_translation
killing_vectors.description: 时间平移 Killing 矢量 (∂_t)，对应能量守恒

killing_vectors.name: rot_phi
killing_vectors.description: 绕 φ 轴旋转 Killing 矢量 (∂_φ)，对应角动量守恒
